FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Interface IImageDecoder

Namespace
FluentGpu.Scene
Assembly
FluentGpu.Scene.dll

The decode seam: the portable cache asks a leaf to decode a source to a target size, off the UI thread. The Windows leaf is WIC→GPU (needs-pixels); the headless leaf is deterministic. Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains completions onto the UI thread (the +1-frame latency contract: a request is never ready the same frame), invoking onPixels with the bucket pixels then onComplete with the state transition.

public interface IImageDecoder

Methods

Begin(int, string, int, int, ImagePriority) 

void Begin(int id, string source, int targetW, int targetH, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible)

Parameters

id int
source string
targetW int
targetH int
priority ImagePriority

Cancel(int)

Cancel an in-flight (or queued) decode — e.g. a virtualized row recycled off-screen. Idempotent.

void Cancel(int id)

Parameters

id int

Prioritize(int, ImagePriority)

Raise the priority of a queued decode (e.g. a prefetch that just scrolled into view). Idempotent; no-op once the job has started.

void Prioritize(int id, ImagePriority priority)

Parameters

id int
priority ImagePriority

Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler)

Drain finished decodes. onPixels gets the bucket pixels (transient span); onComplete gets the final state transition incl. ImageFailureKind + attempt count (after any transient retries the decoder performed internally).

void Pump(ImageCompleteHandler onComplete, ImageReadyHandler onPixels)

Parameters

onComplete ImageCompleteHandler
onPixels ImageReadyHandler
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
